text: Code refactoring means improving the internal structure of your code without changing what it does. Imagine cleaning out your closet: you don't throw away your clothes, but you organise them so that everything is easier to find. Similarly, refactoring makes the code cleaner and more readable for other developers. It reduces technical debt—the hidden cost of messy, rushed code that slows progress over time. By regularly refactoring, you ensure that the codebase stays efficient and easy to modify, which saves time and effort in the long run.
